name: Cream Cheese Swirled Brownies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (180°C). Grease a 13 x 9 x 2-inch (3.5 L) pan with shortening.
2. For the filling, cream together the Golden Crisco Shortening, cream cheese, and vanilla in a small mixing bowl at medium speed of an electric mixer until well blended. Beat in 1/2 cup (125 mL) sugar. Add 2 eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition. Beat in 3 tablespoons (45 mL) flour. Set aside.
3. For the brownie, melt the Golden Crisco Shortening and chocolate in a large saucepan on low heat. Remove from heat. Stir 2 cups (500 mL) sugar into the melted chocolate mixture in the saucepan. Add 4 eggs, one at a time, stirring each egg quickly into the hot mixture. Stir in 1 teaspoon (5 mL) vanilla.
4. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt. Gradually stir into the chocolate mixture.
5. Spread half of the brownie mixture in the pan. Drop the cream cheese mixture over the surface of the brownie layer. Spread gently to cover. Drop the remaining brownie mixture over the cream cheese layer. Spread gently to cover. Swirl the two mixtures together using the tip of a knife (Hint: A nice swirl design depends on how much you pull the knife through the batter; do not overdo it).
6. Bake at 350°F (180°C) for 35 minutes. Allow to cool completely before cutting into squares about 2 x 2 inches (5 cm x 5 cm). Makes: 2 dozen squares. Preparation Time: 20 minutes.
TIP: For decorating with chocolate, melt chocolate on very low heat or medium power in your microwave.
